---
title: "SCP Project"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-10"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(haven)
library(dplyr)
library(lmtest)
library(sandwich)
library(stargazer)
library(ggplot2)

df_longdata <- readRDS(
  path.expand("~/python-venv-demo/SCP-project/Data/UKHLS_long_hijklmno.rds")
)
```


```{r}
# Clean variables and construct main analysis variables
df <- df_longdata %>%
  mutate(
    # Outcomes
    ghq_likert   = ifelse(scghq1_dv < 0, NA, scghq1_dv),
    ghq_caseness = ifelse(scghq2_dv < 0, NA, scghq2_dv),

    # Geography and timing
    country    = ifelse(country < 0, NA, country),
    intdaty_dv = ifelse(intdaty_dv < 0, NA, intdaty_dv),
    intdatm_dv = ifelse(intdatm_dv < 0, NA, intdatm_dv),

    # Children
    nkids05   = ifelse(nkids05 < 0, NA, nkids05),
    nkids615  = ifelse(nkids615 < 0, NA, nkids615),
    nkids015  = ifelse(nkids015 < 0, NA, nkids015),
    nkids_dv  = ifelse(nkids_dv < 0, NA, nkids_dv),

    # Benefits
    benbase1 = ifelse(benbase1 < 0, NA, benbase1),
    benbase2 = ifelse(benbase2 < 0, NA, benbase2),
    benbase3 = ifelse(benbase3 < 0, NA, benbase3),
    benbase4 = ifelse(benbase4 < 0, NA, benbase4),
    benctc   = ifelse(benctc < 0, NA, benctc),

    # Demographics / controls
    age_dv      = ifelse(age_dv < 0, NA, age_dv),
    sex_dv      = ifelse(sex_dv < 0, NA, sex_dv),
    jbstat      = ifelse(jbstat < 0, NA, jbstat),
    hhtype_dv   = ifelse(hhtype_dv < 0, NA, hhtype_dv),
    marstat_dv  = ifelse(marstat_dv < 0, NA, marstat_dv),
    ethn_dv     = ifelse(ethn_dv < 0, NA, ethn_dv),
    health = ifelse(health < 0, NA, health),

    # Convenience timing vars
    year  = intdaty_dv,
    month = intdatm_dv,

    # Labour market
    employed = case_when(
      is.na(jbstat) ~ NA_real_,
      jbstat %in% c(1, 2) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    # Gender
    female = case_when(
      is.na(sex_dv) ~ NA_real_,
      sex_dv == 2 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),
    
    #Ethinicty
    white_ethnicity = case_when(
      is.na(ethn_dv) ~ NA_real_,
      ethn_dv %in% c(1, 2, 4) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    # Household structure
    partnered = case_when(
      is.na(marstat_dv) ~ NA_real_,
      marstat_dv %in% c(1, 2) ~ 1,   # married/civil partner or living as couple
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    # Scotland dummy
    scotland = case_when(
      is.na(country) ~ NA_real_,
      country == 3 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    # Benefit eligibility proxy
    benefit_eligible = case_when(
      is.na(benbase1) & is.na(benbase2) & is.na(benbase4) ~ NA_real_,
      benbase4 == 1 | benbase1 == 1 | benbase2 == 1 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    # Full-rollout child eligibility (all children under 16)
    child_eligible = case_when(
      is.na(nkids015) ~ NA_real_,
      nkids015 > 0 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    # Final eligibility proxy
    eligible = case_when(
      is.na(benefit_eligible) | is.na(child_eligible) ~ NA_real_,
      benefit_eligible == 1 & child_eligible == 1 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    # Policy timing: full rollout / expansion from Nov 2022
    post = case_when(
      is.na(year) | is.na(month) ~ NA_real_,
      year > 2022 | (year == 2022 & month >= 11) ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    # Child/family controls
    has_young_kids = case_when(
      is.na(nkids05) ~ NA_real_,
      nkids05 > 0 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    larger_family = case_when(
      is.na(nkids015) ~ NA_real_,
      nkids015 >= 3 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),

    # Broad age bands
    age_band = case_when(
      is.na(age_dv) ~ NA_character_,
      age_dv <= 25 ~ "18-25",
      age_dv <= 30 ~ "26-30",
      age_dv <= 35 ~ "31-35",
      age_dv <= 40 ~ "36-40",
      TRUE ~ "41-65"
    ),

    # Long-term illness / disability dummy
    disability = case_when(
      is.na(health) ~ NA_real_,
      health == 1 ~ 1,
      health == 2 ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    #quarterly dummies for event study
    quarter = case_when(
      month %in% 1:3   ~ "Q1",
      month %in% 4:6   ~ "Q2",
      month %in% 7:9   ~ "Q3",
      month %in% 10:12 ~ "Q4"
    ),
    yq = paste0(year, "_", quarter),
    yq.f = factor(yq)
  )


na_counts <- colSums(is.na(df))
na_counts[order(-na_counts)]

na_share <- colMeans(is.na(df))
na_share[order(-na_share)]
```
```{r}
# Analysis sample: working-age parents in Scotland vs England only
df_analysis <- df %>%
  filter(
    age_dv >= 18,
    age_dv <= 65,

    # Parents only
    !is.na(nkids015),
    nkids015 > 0,

    # Geography: Scotland (3) and England (1) only
    country %in% c(1, 3),

    # Core variables must be non-missing
    !is.na(ghq_likert),
    !is.na(scotland),
    !is.na(eligible),
    !is.na(year),
    !is.na(month),

    # Optional but recommended: ensure key controls are non-missing
    !is.na(female),
    !is.na(employed),
    !is.na(partnered),
    !is.na(disability),
    !is.na(white_ethnicity),

    # Drop COVID disruption period
    !(year == 2020 & month >= 3),
    !(year == 2021 & month <= 6)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Young children indicator (keep here or upstream — fine either way)
    has_young_kids = case_when(
      is.na(nkids05) ~ NA_real_,
      nkids05 > 0 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    )
  )
```


```{r}
# Summary statistics for analysis sample
summary_stats <- df_analysis %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),

    ghq_mean = mean(ghq_likert, na.rm = TRUE),
    ghq_sd   = sd(ghq_likert, na.rm = TRUE),

    age_mean = mean(age_dv, na.rm = TRUE),
    age_sd   = sd(age_dv, na.rm = TRUE),

    female_share      = mean(female, na.rm = TRUE),
    employed_share    = mean(employed, na.rm = TRUE),
    partnered_share   = mean(partnered, na.rm = TRUE),
    white_ethnicity_share = mean(white_ethnicity, na.rm = TRUE),

    young_kids_share  = mean(has_young_kids, na.rm = TRUE),
    larger_family_share = mean(larger_family, na.rm = TRUE),

    benefit_share     = mean(benefit_eligible, na.rm = TRUE),
    disability_share  = mean(disability, na.rm = TRUE),
    scotland_share    = mean(scotland, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

summary_stats
```


```{r}
# Summary statistics by country
summary_by_country <- df_analysis %>%
  group_by(scotland) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),

    ghq_mean = mean(ghq_likert, na.rm = TRUE),
    age_mean = mean(age_dv, na.rm = TRUE),

    female_share      = mean(female, na.rm = TRUE),
    employed_share    = mean(employed, na.rm = TRUE),
    partnered_share   = mean(partnered, na.rm = TRUE),
    white_ethnicity_share = mean(white_ethnicity, na.rm = TRUE),

    young_kids_share    = mean(has_young_kids, na.rm = TRUE),
    larger_family_share = mean(larger_family, na.rm = TRUE),

    benefit_share    = mean(benefit_eligible, na.rm = TRUE),
    disability_share = mean(disability, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

summary_by_country
```

```{r}
# Summary statistics by country and post period
summary_by_country_post <- df_analysis %>%
  group_by(scotland, post) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),

    ghq_mean = mean(ghq_likert, na.rm = TRUE),
    age_mean = mean(age_dv, na.rm = TRUE),

    female_share      = mean(female, na.rm = TRUE),
    employed_share    = mean(employed, na.rm = TRUE),
    partnered_share   = mean(partnered, na.rm = TRUE),
    white_ethnicity_share = mean(white_ethnicity, na.rm = TRUE),

    young_kids_share    = mean(has_young_kids, na.rm = TRUE),
    larger_family_share = mean(larger_family, na.rm = TRUE),

    benefit_share    = mean(benefit_eligible, na.rm = TRUE),
    disability_share = mean(disability, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary_by_country_post$scotland <- ifelse(summary_by_country_post$scotland == 1, "Scotland", "England")
summary_by_country_post$post <- ifelse(summary_by_country_post$post == 1, "Post-Nov 2022", "Pre-Nov 2022")

summary_by_country_post
```

We now run the baseline DiD:

```{r}
# create factors
df_analysis$year.f = factor(df_analysis$year)
# Create factors
df_analysis$year.f   <- factor(df_analysis$year)
df_analysis$region.f <- factor(df_analysis$gor_dv)
df_analysis$age_band <- factor(df_analysis$age_band)

#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~#
#          Main DiD              #
#~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~#

# (1) Baseline: minimal FE model
reg1.all <- lm(
  ghq_likert ~ scotland*eligible*post + region.f + year.f,
  data = df_analysis
)

reg1 <- coeftest(reg1.all, vcovCL(reg1.all, cluster = ~ gor_dv))


# (2) Preferred specification (FULL CONTROLS)
reg2.all <- lm(
  ghq_likert ~ scotland*eligible*post +
    female +
    employed +
    partnered +
    larger_family +
    has_young_kids +
    white_ethnicity +
    disability +
    age_band +
    region.f + year.f,
  data = df_analysis
)

reg2 <- coeftest(reg2.all, vcovCL(reg2.all, cluster = ~ gor_dv))


# Output
stargazer(reg1, reg2,
          keep = c("scotland:eligible:post"),
          type = "text")

# Sample sizes
cat("N reg1:", nobs(reg1.all), "\n")
cat("N reg2:", nobs(reg2.all), "\n")
```

```{r}
reg_sp.all <- lm(
  ghq_likert ~ scotland * eligible * post * partnered +
    female + employed + partnered +
    larger_family + has_young_kids +
    white_ethnicity + disability + age_band +
    region.f + year.f,
  data = df_analysis
)

reg_sp <- coeftest(reg_sp.all, vcovCL(reg_sp.all, cluster = ~ gor_dv))

stargazer(reg_sp,
  keep = c(
    "scotland:eligible:post",
    "scotland:eligible:post:partnered"
  ),
  type = "text"
)
```

```{r}

# Set omitted pre-treatment quarter
df_analysis$yq.f <- relevel(df_analysis$yq.f, ref = "2019_Q4")

# Region factor
df_analysis$region.f <- factor(df_analysis$gor_dv)

es_qtr.all <- lm(
  ghq_likert ~ 
    scotland:eligible:yq.f +   # coefficients of interest
    scotland:yq.f +            # Scotland-specific quarter effects
    eligible:yq.f +            # Eligible-group-specific quarter effects
    scotland + eligible +
    female + employed + partnered +
    larger_family + has_young_kids +
    white_ethnicity + disability + age_band +
    region.f,
  data = df_analysis
)

es_qtr <- coeftest(es_qtr.all, vcovCL(es_qtr.all, cluster = ~ gor_dv))

# Show only event-study coefficients
es_names <- grep("^scotland:eligible:yq.f", rownames(es_qtr), value = TRUE)

stargazer(es_qtr, keep = es_names, type = "text")
  
```

```{r}
#-----------------------------------------------#
# 3. Prepare data for plot
#-----------------------------------------------#

es_coef <- es_qtr[es_names, 1]
es_se   <- es_qtr[es_names, 2]

es_period <- gsub("scotland:eligible:yq.f", "", es_names)

fig.dat <- data.frame(
  period = es_period,
  coef   = es_coef,
  se     = es_se
)

# Order periods correctly
period_levels <- levels(df_analysis$yq.f)
period_levels <- period_levels[period_levels != "2019_Q4"]  # omitted category not plotted

fig.dat$period <- factor(fig.dat$period, levels = period_levels)

#-----------------------------------------------#
# 4. Plot
#-----------------------------------------------#

ggplot(fig.dat, aes(x = period, y = coef, group = 1)) +
  geom_pointrange(
    aes(ymin = coef - 1.96 * se,
        ymax = coef + 1.96 * se),
    size = 0.4,
    shape = 20
  ) +
  geom_line(linetype = "longdash") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, alpha = 0.3, size = 0.5) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  ylab("Effect on GHQ score") +
  xlab("Quarter (relative to 2019 Q4 omitted)") +
  ggtitle("Quarterly event study: SCP and mental health")
```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```
